methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $96M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

private.name: Type One Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Series B), post-money $200M+

data.narrative:
Antares is a Series B nuclear energy company founded by Jordan Bramble and Julia DeWahl that has raised $96M+ to date. The nuclear energy sector includes 3 public comparables (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private peers spanning advanced reactor and fuel technologies, including Type One Energy, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power.

Stage-progression analysis, the primary applicable method here, uses Antares' $96M total raised as a proxy for current valuation and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the next financing stage. This yields an implied valuation of $182M. A sensitivity range anchored to this result, with conservative and aggressive adjustments, supports a low of $155M and a high of $210M.

Peer-median valuation could not be applied due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables in nuclear energy—the sector's limited number of mature private companies at comparable funding levels prevented reliable triangulation via this method. The analysis therefore rests on stage-progression alone. Key uncertainties include the appropriate step-up multiplier for frontier nuclear (which may vary by sub-sector maturity), the relevance of public-market valuations given sector volatility, and the timing and size of Antares' next funding event. The $155M–$210M range reflects the stage-based implied value with acknowledged reliance on a single method.
